details: Dependency-confusion attack: @one-chat/react@99.9.1 is a 354-byte stub package (empty index.js) with a single dependency "ltidisafe" pointing to an off-registry CDN URL (hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3[.]2[.]8[.]tgz). The sentinel version 99.9.1 and scoped name are designed to be installed in place of an internal/private package. When installed, npm fetches the attacker-controlled tarball from the CDN as a transitive dependency, delivering the payload without any lifecycle hook. Seven dependency-confusion packages published at version 99.9.1, each an empty stub (no code, no lifecycle hooks) that declares a single dependency on a remote tarball hosted at ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-*.tgz. The scoped names (@meli-testing/jest-react, @nordic-dev/linting-tools, @fuji-web-components/maps, @mplay-core-lib/utilities, @mplay-frontend-ui/link, @global-theme/context, @one-chat/react) mimic internal/private organization packages, and the sentinel version 99.9.1 ensures they win dependency resolution. When installed, npm fetches and extracts the remote ltidisafe tarball from the Google Cloud Storage bucket, which is the actual payload. The remote tarball URLs are: hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3[.]2[.]4[.]tgz, hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3[.]2[.]7[.]tgz, hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3[.]2[.]3[.]tgz, and similar version variants across the packages.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
